Replaces round-robin with latency-aware selection using EWMA-smoothed\n4\t//! metrics: latency p95, in-flight request count, and error rate.\n5\t\n6\tuse crate::error::{MiroirError, Result};\n7\tuse \"snake_case\")]\n18\tpub enum SelectionStrategy {\n19\t    /// EWMA-based adaptive selection.\n20\t    Adaptive,\n21\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n22\t    RoundRobin,\n23\t    /// Random #[serde(default = \"default_inflight_weight\")]\n44\t    pub inflight_weight: f64,\n45\t    /// Error rate weight.\n46\t    #[serde(default = \"default_error_weight\")]\n47\t    pub error_weight: \"adaptive\".into(),\n76\t            latency_weight: default_latency_weight(),\n77\t            inflight_weight: default_inflight_weight(),\n78\t            error_weight: default_error_weight(),\n79\t            ewma_half_life_ms: default_ewma_half_life(),\n80\t            exploration_epsilon: default_epsilon(),\n81\t        }\n82\t    }\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\t/// Per-node metrics for adaptive selection.\n86\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n87\tpub struct NodeMetrics {\n88\t    /// EWMA of latency p95 in milliseconds.\n89\t    pub latency_p95_ms: f64,\n90\t    /// Current in-flight request count.\n91\t    pub in_flight: u32,\n92\t    /// EWMA of error rate (0.0 to 1.0).\n93\t    pub error_rate: f64,\n94\t    /// EWMA half-life for updates.\n95\t    pub half_life_ms: u64,\n96\t    /// Last update timestamp.\n97\t    pub last_updated: Instant,\n98\t}\n99\t\n100\timpl NodeMetrics {\n101\t    /// Create new metrics with initial values.\n102\t    pub fn new(initial_latency_ms: f64, half_life_ms: u64) -> Self {\n103\t        Self {\n104\t            latency_p95_ms: initial_latency_ms,\n105\t            in_flight: 0,\n106\t            error_rate: 0.0,\n107\t            half_life_ms,\n108\t            last_updated: Instant::now(),\n109\t        }\n110\t    }\n111\t\n112\t    /// Update latency with EWMA smoothing.\n113\t    pub fn update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n114\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n115\t        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n116\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n120\t    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n121\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n122\t        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n123\t        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n124\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n125\t    }\n126\t\n127\t    /// Increment in-flight count.\n128\t    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n129\t        self.in_flight += 1;\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n133\t    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n134\t        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n135\t    }\n136\t\n137\t    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n138\t    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n139\t        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n140\t            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n141\t            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n142\t    }\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\timpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n146\t    fn default() -> Self {\n147\t        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Replica selector.\n152\tpub struct ReplicaSelector {\n153\t    /// Configuration.\n154\t    config: ReplicaSelectionConfig,\n155\t    /// Per-node metrics.\n156\t    metrics: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<NodeId, NodeMetrics>>>,\n157\t    /// Round-robin counter (for round-robin strategy).\n158\t    rr_counter: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<String, u64>>>,\n159\t    /// Random number generator.\n160\t    rng: Arc<std::sync::Mutex<StdRng>>,\n161\t}\n162\t\n163\timpl ReplicaSelector {\n164\t    /// Create a new replica selector.\n165\t    pub fn new(config: ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> Self {\n166\t        Self {\n167\t            config,\n168\t            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n169\t            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n170\t            rng: Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(StdRng::from_entropy())),\n171\t        }\n172\t    }\n173\t\n174\t    /// Select a node from the given candidates.\n175\t    ///\n176\t    /// Returns the selected node ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n177\t    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n178\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n179\t            return None;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n183\t\n184\t        match strategy {\n185\t            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n186\t            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n187\t            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n188\t        }\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n192\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n193\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n194\t\n195\t        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n196\t        if self.should_explore() {\n197\t            return self.select_random(candidates);\n198\t        }\n199\t\n200\t        // Compute scores and find the minimum\n201\t        let mut best_node = None;\n202\t        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n203\t\n204\t        for node in candidates {\n205\t            let score = metrics\n206\t                .get(node)\n207\t                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n208\t                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n209\t\n210\t            if score < best_score {\n211\t                best_score = score;\n212\t                best_node = Some(node.clone());\n213\t            }\n214\t        }\n215\t\n216\t        best_node\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n220\t    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n221\t        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n222\t        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n223\t        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n224\t        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n225\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    /// Random selection.\n229\t    fn select_random(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n230\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n231\t            return None;\n232\t        }\n233\t        let idx = self\n234\t            .rng\n235\t            .lock()\n236\t            .unwrap()\n237\t            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n238\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n239\t    }\n240\t\n241\t    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n242\t    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n243\t        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n244\t        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n248\t    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n249\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n250\t        let entry = metrics\n251\t            .entry(node.clone())\n252\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n253\t        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n254\t        entry.update_error(false);\n255\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    /// Record a failed request.\n259\t    pub async fn record_error(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: Option<f64>) {\n260\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n261\t        let entry = metrics\n262\t            .entry(node.clone())\n263\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n264\t        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n265\t            entry.update_latency(lat);\n266\t        }\n267\t {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_b8432481049447c59ec36087","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t//! Rendezvous hash-based routing and shard assignment.\n2\t\n3\tuse crate::migration::{MigrationCoordinator, ShardId};\n4\tuse crate::topology::{Group, NodeId, Topology};\n5\tuse std::collections::HashSet;\n6\tuse std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};\n7\tuse twox_hash::XxHash64;\n8\t\n9\t/// Compute a rendezvous score for a shard+node pair.\n10\t///\n11\t/// Higher scores win; used for deterministic shard assignment.\n12\tpub fn score(shard_id: u32, node_id: &str) -> u64 {\n13\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n14\t    shard_id.hash(&mut h);\n15\t    node_id.hash(&mut h);\n16\t    h.finish()\n17\t}\n18\t\n19\t/// Assign a shard to `rf` nodes within a single replica group.\n20\t///\n21\t/// `group_nodes` is the subset of nodes belonging to that group.\n22\t///\n23\t/// Sorts by score descending, breaking ties lexicographically on node_id\n24\t/// for deterministic resolution.\n25\tpub fn assign_shard_in_group(shard_id: u32, group_nodes: &[NodeId], rf: usize) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n26\t    let mut scored: Vec<(u64, &NodeId)> = group_nodes\n27\t        .iter()\n28\t        .map(|n| (score(shard_id, n.as_str()), n))\n29\t        .collect();\n30\t    scored.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {\n31\t        b.0.cmp(&a.0)\n32\t            .then_with(|| a.1.as_str().cmp(b.1.as_str()))\n33\t    });\n34\t    scored\n35\t        .into_iter()\n36\t        .take(rf)\n37\t        .map(|(_, n)| n.clone())\n38\t        .collect()\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// All write targets for a document: the RF nodes in EACH replica group.\n42\tpub fn write_targets(shard_id: u32, topology: &Topology) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n43\t    topology\n44\t        .groups()\n45\t        .flat_map(|group| assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf()))\n46\t        .collect()\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// All write targets for a document, considering dual-write state during migration.\n50\t///\n51\t/// This is the migration-aware version of `write_targets`. When a shard is in\n52\t/// dual-write phase (node addition in progress), it includes both the old owner\n53\t/// AND the new node in the target list to ensure no writes are lost during migration.\n54\t///\n55\t/// # Arguments\n56\t/// * `shard_id` - The shard ID being written to\n57\t/// * `topology` - The cluster topology\n58\t/// * `migration_coordinator` - Optional migration coordinator for dual-write detection\n59\t///\n60\t/// # Returns\n61\t/// A vector of node IDs that should receive the write. During dual-write for a shard,\n62\t/// this includes both the standard RF nodes AND the new node.\n63\tpub fn write_targets_with_migration(\n64\t    shard_id: u32,\n65\t    topology: &Topology,\n66\t    migration_coordinator: Option<&MigrationCoordinator>,\n67\t) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n68\t    let shard = ShardId(shard_id);\n69\t\n70\t    // Start with standard write targets\n71\t    let mut targets: Vec<NodeId> = write_targets(shard_id, topology);\n72\t\n73\t    // Check if this shard is in dual-write phase\n74\t    if let Some(coordinator) = migration_coordinator {\n75\t        if coordinator.is_dual_write_active(shard) {\n76\t            // Find migrations affecting this shard\n77\t            for (_mid, state) in coordinator.get_all_migrations() {\n78\t                if state.affected_shards.contains_key(&shard) {\n79\t                    // This shard is being migrated - include the new node\n80\t                    // Convert migration NodeId to topology NodeId\n81\t                    let new_node_id = crate::topology::NodeId::new(state.new_node.0.clone());\n82\t\n83\t                    // Only add if not already in targets\n84\t                    if !targets.contains(&new_node_id) {\n85\t                        targets.push(new_node_id);\n86\t                    }\n87\t                }\n88\t            }\n89\t        }\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    targets\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t/// Select the replica group for a query (round-robin by query counter).\n96\t///\n97\t/// Returns 0 when there are no replica groups (caller handles the empty case).\n98\tpub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\n99\t    if replica_groups == 0 {\n100\t        return 0;\n101\t    }\n102\t    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\n103\t}\n104\t\n105\t/// The covering set for a search: one node per shard within the chosen group.\n106\tpub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\n107\t    (0..shard_count)\n108\t        .map(|shard_id| {\n109\t            let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n110\t            // rotate through replicas for intra-group load balancing\n111\t            replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\n112\t        })\n113\t        .collect::<HashSet<_>>()\n114\t        .into_iter()\n115\t        .collect()\n116\t}\n117\t\n118\t/// Covering set with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n119\t///\n120\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n121\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n122\tpub fn covering_set_with_version_floor(\n123\t    shard_count: u32,\n124\t    group: &Group,\n125\t    rf: usize,\n126\t    query_seq: u64,\n127\t    index: &str,\n128\t    floor: u64,\n129\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n130\t) -> Option<Vec<NodeId>> {\n131\t    let mut result = Vec::new();\n132\t    let mut used_nodes = HashSet::new();\n133\t\n134\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n135\t        let replicas = assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n136\t\n137\t        // Filter replicas by settings version floor, then by query_seq rotation\n138\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n139\t            .iter()\n140\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n141\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n142\t                version >= floor\n143\t            })\n144\t            .collect();\n145\t\n146\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n147\t            // No eligible replica for this shard\n148\t            return None;\n149\t        }\n150\t\n151\t        // Rotate through eligible replicas using query_seq\n152\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n153\t        if used_nodes.insert(selected.clone()) {\n154\t            result.push(selected.clone());\n155\t        }\n156\t    }\n157\t\n158\t    Some(result)\n159\t}\n160\t\n161\t/// Compute the shard ID for a document's primary key.\n162\tpub fn shard_for_key(primary_key: &str, shard_count: u32) -> u32 {\n163\t    let mut h = XxHash64::with_seed(0);\n164\t    primary_key.hash(&mut h);\n165\t    (h.finish() % shard_count as u64) as u32\n166\t}\n167\t\n168\t/// Count differences between two shard assignments.\n169\t///\n170\t/// Returns the number of shard-node pairs that differ between old and new.\n171\t/// For each shard, {\n176\t    let old_map: std::collections::HashMap<_, _> = old_shards\n177\t        .iter()\n178\t        .map(|(shard, nodes)| (*shard, nodes.clone()))\n179\t        .collect();\n180\t\n181\t    let change once)\n185\t            for node in new_nodes {\n186\t                if !old_nodes.contains(node) {\n187\t                    diff += 1;\n188\t                }\n189\t            }\n190\t        }\n191\t    }\n192\t    diff\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t#[cfg(test)]\n196\tmod tests {\n197\t    use super::*;\n198\t    use crate::topology::{Node, NodeId};\n199\t    use  /// Test tie-breaking in assign_shard_in_group.\n399\t    #[test]\n400\t    fn test_tie_breaking() {\n401\t        // Use node IDs that are unlikely but could theoretically collide\n402\t        let nodes = vec![\n403\t            NodeId::new(\"node-zzz\".to_string()),\n404\t            NodeId::new(\"node-aaa\".to_string()),\n405\t        ];\n406\t\n407\t        let result = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n408\t\n409\t        // Even if scores were identical (extremely rare), node-aaa should come first\n410\t        // because we sort by node_id ascending for ties\n411\t        assert_eq!(result.len(), 1);\n412\t        // The actual result depends on hash values - we're just verifying determinism\n413\t        let result2 = assign_shard_in_group(0, &nodes, 1);\n414\t        assert_eq!(result, result2, \"Tie-breaking is non-deterministic\");\n415\t    }\n416\t\n417\t    /// Test score function uses canonical hash order.\n418\t    #[test]\n419\t    fn test_score_canonical_order() {\n420\t        let node_a = \"node-a\";\n421\t        let node_b = \"node-b\";\n422\t\n423\t        // Different shard_id → different score\n424\t        let score_0_a = score(0, node_a);\n425\t        let score_1_a = score(1, node_a);\n426\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_1_a, \"Same node, different shard should differ\");\n427\t\n428\t        // Different node_id → different score (same shard)\n429\t        let score_0_b = score(0, node_b);\n430\t        assert_ne!(score_0_a, score_0_b, \"Same shard, different node should differ\");\n431\t\n432\t        // Verify determinism\n433\t        assert_eq!(score(0, node_a), score_0_a, \"Score is non-deterministic\");\n434\t    }\n435\t\n436\t    // ── P1.3 acceptance tests: P1.3-A6: query_group distribution is uniform (chi-square test).\n595\t    #[test]\n596\t    fn test_query_group_uniform_distribution() {\n597\t        let replica_groups = 5u32;\n598\t        let samples = 10_000;\n599\t\n600\t        let mut counts = vec![0usize; replica_groups as usize];\n601\t        for query_seq in 0..samples {\n602\t            let g = query_group(query_seq as u64, replica_groups);\n603\t            counts[g as usize] += 1;\n604\t        }\n605\t\n606\t        // Expected count per group: samples / RG\n607\t        let expected = samples as f64 / replica_groups as f64;\n608\t\n609\t        // Chi-square statistic: sum((observed - expected)^2 / expected)\n610\t        let chi_square: f64 = counts\n611\t            .iter()\n612\t            .map(|&observed| {\n613\t                let diff = observed as f64 - expected;\n614\t                (diff * diff) / expected\n615\t            })\n616\t            .sum();\n617\t\n618\t        // Degrees of freedom = RG - 1 = 4\n619\t        // Critical value at p=0.95 is ~9.49\n620\t        let critical_value = 9.49;\n621\t\n622\t        assert!(\n623\t            chi_square < critical_value,\n624\t            \"query_group distribution not uniform: chi-square={} > {}\",\n625\t            chi_square,\n626\t            critical_value\n627\t        );\n628\t    }\n629\t\n630\t    /// P1.3-A7: covering_set covering_set(8, group, 3, 2);\n646\t\n647\t        // For each shard, verify that the selected node rotates\n648\t        for shard_id in 0..8 {\n649\t            let replicas = assert!(c2.contains(r2), \"query_seq=2 should select third replica\");\n657\t        }\n658\t (dfs_query_then_fetch pattern)\n25\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n26\t\n27\t/// Per-term document frequency from a single shard.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n29\tpub struct TermStats {\n30\t    pub df: u64,\n31\t}\n32\t\n33\t/// Preflight request: gather term-frequency statistics from a shard.\n34\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n35\tpub struct PreflightRequest {\n36\t    pub index_uid: String,\n37\t    pub terms: Vec<String>,\n38\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n39\t}\n40\t\n41\t/// Response from a shard's preflight query.\n42\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n43\tpub struct PreflightResponse {\n44\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n45\t    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n46\t    pub term_stats: HashMap<String, TermStats>,\n47\t}\n48\t\n49\t/// Aggregated global term statistics after coordinator aggregation.\n50\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n51\tpub struct GlobalTermStats {\n52\t    pub df: u64,\n53\t    pub idf: f64,\n54\t}\n55\t\n56\t/// Aggregated global IDF data computed at the coordinator.\n57\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n58\tpub struct GlobalIdf {\n59\t    pub total_docs: u64,\n60\t    pub avg_doc_length: f64,\n61\t    pub terms: HashMap<String, GlobalTermStats>,\n62\t}\n63\t\n64\timpl GlobalIdf {\n65\t    /// Aggregate per-shard preflight responses into global IDF.\n66\t    pub fn from_preflight_responses(responses: &[PreflightResponse]) -> Self {\n67\t        let mut total_docs = 0u64;\n68\t        let mut total_length = 0.0f64;\n69\t        let mut term_df: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();\n70\t\n71\t        for resp in responses {\n72\t            total_docs += resp.total_docs;\n73\t            total_length += resp.avg_doc_length * resp.total_docs as f64;\n74\t            for (term, stats) in &resp.term_stats {\n75\t                *term_df.entry(term.clone()).or_insert(0) += stats.df;\n76\t            }\n77\t        }\n78\t\n79\t        let avg_doc_length = if total_docs > 0 {\n80\t            total_length / total_docs as f64\n81\t        } else {\n82\t            0.0\n83\t        };\n84\t\n85\t        let n = total_docs as f64;\n86\t        let terms = term_df\n87\t            .into_iter()\n88\t            .map(|(term, df)| {\n89\t                let idf = if df == 0 {\n90\t                    0.0\n91\t                } else {\n92\t                    ((n - df as f64 + 0.5) / (df as f64 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0\n93\t                };\n94\t                (term, GlobalTermStats { df, idf })\n95\t            })\n96\t            .collect();\n97\t\n98\t        Self { total_docs, avg_doc_length, terms }\n99\t    }\n100\t}\n101\t\n102\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n103\t// NodeClient trait\n104\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n105\t\n106\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n107\t// Write path: document operations (P2.2)\n108\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n109\t\n110\t/// Request to add/replace documents on a node.\n111\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n112\tpub struct WriteRequest {\n113\t    pub index_uid: String,\n114\t    pub documents: Vec<Value>,\n115\t    pub primary_key: Option<String>,\n116\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n117\t    /// - None = client write (always emitted to CDC)\n118\t    /// - Some(\"antientropy\") = anti-entropy repair write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n119\t    /// - Some(\"reshard_backfill\") = reshard backfill write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n120\t    /// - Some(\"rollover\") = ILM rollover write (suppressed unless emit_internal_writes)\n121\t    /// - Some(\"ttl_expire\") = TTL expiration delete (suppressed unless emit_ttl_deletes)\n122\t    ///\n123\t    /// This field is NEVER stored on documents and never returned to clients.\n124\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n125\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t/// Response from a single node's document write operation.\n129\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n130\tpub struct WriteResponse {\n131\t    pub success: bool,\n132\t    pub task_uid: Option<u64>,\n133\t    pub message: Option<String>,\n134\t    pub code: Option<String>,\n135\t    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t/// Request to delete documents by IDs.\n139\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n140\tpub struct DeleteByIdsRequest {\n141\t    pub index_uid: String,\n142\t    pub ids: Vec<String>,\n143\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n144\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n145\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n146\t}\n147\t\n148\t/// Request to delete all documents matching a filter.\n149\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n150\tpub struct DeleteByFilterRequest {\n151\t    pub index_uid: String,\n152\t    pub filter: Value,\n153\t    /// Internal origin tag for CDC event suppression (plan §13.13).\n154\t    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n155\t    pub origin: Option<String>,\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t/// Response from a delete operation.\n159\tpub type DeleteResponse = WriteResponse;\n160\t\n161\t/// Request to fetch documents with a filter (used for shard migration).\n162\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n163\tpub struct FetchDocumentsRequest {\n164\t    pub index_uid: String,\n165\t    pub filter: Value,\n166\t    pub limit: u32,\n167\t    pub offset: u32,\n168\t}\n169\t\n170\t/// Response from a fetch documents operation.\n171\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n172\tpub struct FetchDocumentsResponse {\n173\t    pub results: Vec<Value>,\n174\t    pub limit: u32,\n175\t    pub offset: u32,\n176\t    pub total: u64,\n177\t}\n178\t\n179\t/// Request to get task status from a node.\n180\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n181\tpub struct TaskStatusRequest {\n182\t    /// The task UID to query\n183\t    pub task_uid: u64,\n184\t}\n185\t\n186\t/// Response from a single node's task status query.\n187\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n188\tpub struct TaskStatusResponse {\n189\t    /// The task UID\n190\t    pub task_uid: u64,\n191\t    /// Current task status\n192\t    pub status: String,\n193\t    /// Error message if failed\n194\t    pub error: Option<String>,\n195\t    /// Error type if failed\n196\t    #[serde(rename = \"type\")]\n197\t    pub error_type: Option<String>,\n198\t}\n199\t\n200\timpl TaskStatusResponse {\n201\t    /// Convert Meilisearch status string to NodeTaskStatus.\n202\t    pub fn to_node_status(&self) -> crate::task::NodeTaskStatus {\n203\t        match self.status.as_str() {\n204\t            \"enqueued\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n205\t            \"processing\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Processing,\n206\t            \"succeeded\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Succeeded,\n207\t            \"failed\" => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Failed,\n208\t            _ => crate::task::NodeTaskStatus::Enqueued,\n209\t        }\n210\t    }\n211\t}\n212\t\n213\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n214\t// NodeClient trait\n215\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n216\t\n217\t/// HTTP client for communicating with a Meilisearch node.\n218\t#[allow(async_fn_in_trait)]\n219\tpub trait NodeClient: Send + Sync {\n220\t    async fn search_node(\n221\t        &self,\n222\t        node: &NodeId,\n223\t        address: &str,\n224\t        request: &SearchRequest,\n225\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError>;\n226\t\n227\t    /// Execute a preflight request (OP#4 global-IDF phase).\n228\t    async fn preflight_node(\n229\t        &self,\n230\t        _node: &NodeId,\n231\t        _address: &str,\n232\t        _request: &PreflightRequest,\n233\t    ) -> std::result::Result<PreflightResponse, NodeError> {\n234\t        Ok(PreflightResponse { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() })\n235\t    }\n236\t\n237\t    /// Write documents to a node (add/replace).\n238\t    async fn write_documents(\n239\t        &self,\n240\t        _node: &NodeId,\n241\t        _address: &str,\n242\t        _request: &WriteRequest,\n243\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n244\t        Ok(WriteResponse {\n245\t            success: false,\n246\t            task_uid: None,\n247\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n248\t            code: None,\n249\t            error_type: None,\n250\t        })\n251\t    }\n252\t\n253\t    /// Get task status from a node.\n254\t    fn get_task_status(\n255\t        &self,\n256\t        _node: &NodeId,\n257\t        _address: &str,\n258\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n259\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n260\t        async move {\n261\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n262\t                task_uid: _request.task_uid,\n263\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n264\t                error: None,\n265\t                error_type: None,\n266\t            })\n267\t        }\n268\t    }\n269\t\n270\t    /// Delete documents by IDs from a node.\n271\t    async fn delete_documents(\n272\t        &self,\n273\t        _node: &NodeId,\n274\t        _address: &str,\n275\t        _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n276\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n277\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n278\t            success: false,\n279\t            task_uid: None,\n280\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n281\t            code: None,\n282\t            error_type: None,\n283\t        })\n284\t    }\n285\t\n286\t    /// Delete all documents matching a filter from a node.\n287\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n288\t        &self,\n289\t        _node: &NodeId,\n290\t        _address: &str,\n291\t        _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n292\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n293\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n294\t            success: false,\n295\t            task_uid: None,\n296\t            message: Some(\"not implemented\".to_string()),\n297\t            code: None,\n298\t            error_type: None,\n299\t        })\n300\t    }\n301\t\n302\t    /// Fetch documents with a filter from a node (used for shard migration).\n303\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n304\t        &self,\n305\t        _node: &NodeId,\n306\t        _address: &str,\n307\t        _request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n308\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n309\t        Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n310\t            results: Vec::new(),\n311\t            limit: _request.limit,\n312\t            offset: _request.offset,\n313\t            total: 0,\n314\t        })\n315\t    }\n316\t}\n317\t\n318\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n319\tpub enum NodeError {\n320\t    Timeout,\n321\t    HttpError { status: u16, body: String },\n322\t    NetworkError(String),\n323\t}\n324\t\n325\t#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\n326\tpub struct SearchRequest {\n327\t    pub index_uid: String,\n328\t    pub query: Option<String>,\n329\t    pub offset: usize,\n330\t    pub limit: usize,\n331\t    pub filter: Option<Value>,\n332\t    pub facets: Option<Vec<String>>,\n333\t    pub ranking_score: bool,\n334\t    pub body: Value,\n335\t    /// Global IDF data from the preflight phase (OP#4).\n336\t    pub global_idf: Option<GlobalIdf>,\n337\t}\n338\t\n339\timpl SearchRequest {\n340\t    /// Build the request body for sending to a node.\n341\t    ///\n342\t    /// Injects `showRankingScore: true` unconditionally so the merger can global-sort.\n343\t    /// Each node receives `offset + limit` results to ensure the coordinator has enough\n344\t    /// data to apply pagination.\n345\t    pub fn to_node_body(&self) -> Value {\n346\t        let mut body = self.body.clone();\n347\t\n348\t        // Inject showRankingScore: true unconditionally for global sorting\n349\t        body[\"showRankingScore\"] = serde_json::json!(true);\n350\t\n351\t        // Set limit to offset + limit so we get enough results for pagination\n352\t        // (coordinator applies final offset/limit after merging)\n353\t        body[\"limit\"] = serde_json::json!(self.offset + self.limit);\n354\t\n355\t        // Set offset to 0 on individual nodes (coordinator handles offset)\n356\t        body[\"offset\"] = serde_json::json!(0);\n357\t\n358\t        // Ensure query is set\n359\t        if let Some(q) = &self.query {\n360\t            body[\"q\"] = serde_json::json!(q);\n361\t        }\n362\t\n363\t        // Ensure filter is set if provided\n364\t        if let Some(filter) = &self.filter {\n365\t            body[\"filter\"] = filter.clone();\n366\t        }\n367\t\n368\t        // Ensure facets are set if provided\n369\t        if let Some(facets) = &self.facets {\n370\t            body[\"facets\"] = serde_json::json!(facets);\n371\t        }\n372\t\n373\t        body\n374\t    }\n375\t}\n376\t\n377\t#[derive(Debug)]\n378\tpub struct ScatterResult {\n379\t    pub shard_pages: Vec<ShardHitPage>,\n380\t    pub failed_shards: HashMap<u32, NodeError>,\n381\t    pub partial: bool,\n382\t    pub deadline_exceeded: bool,\n383\t}\n384\t\n385\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(query_seq, rf, shard_count))]\n386\tpub fn plan_search_scatter(\n387\t    topology: &Topology,\n388\t    query_seq: u64,\n389\t    rf: usize,\n390\t    shard_count: u32,\n391\t) -> ScatterPlan {\n392\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n393\t\n394\t    let group = match topology.group(chosen_group) {\n395\t        Some(g) => g,\n396\t        None => {\n397\t            return ScatterPlan {\n398\t                chosen_group, target_shards: Vec::new(),\n399\t                shard_to_node: HashMap::new(), deadline_ms: 0, hedging_eligible: false,\n400\t            };\n401\t        }\n402\t    };\n403\t\n404\t    let _covering = covering_set(shard_count, group, rf, query_seq);\n405\t\n406\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n407\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n408\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n409\t        let selected = replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone();\n410\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n411\t    }\n412\t\n413\t    ScatterPlan {\n414\t        chosen_group,\n415\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n416\t        shard_to_node,\n417\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n418\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n419\t    }\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t/// Plan search scatter with settings version floor filtering (plan §13.5).\n423\t///\n424\t/// Excludes nodes whose settings version for the given index is below `floor`.\n425\t/// Returns None if no covering set can be assembled (caller should return 503).\n426\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_with_version_floor(\n427\t    topology: &Topology,\n428\t    query_seq: u64,\n429\t    rf: usize,\n430\t    shard_count: u32,\n431\t    index: &str,\n432\t    floor: u64,\n433\t    version_checker: &impl Fn(&str, &str) -> u64,\n434\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n435\t    let chosen_group = query_group(query_seq, topology.replica_group_count());\n436\t\n437\t    let group = topology.group(chosen_group)?;\n438\t\n439\t    let covering = covering_set_with_version_floor(\n440\t        shard_count,\n441\t        group,\n442\t        rf,\n443\t        query_seq,\n444\t        index,\n445\t        floor,\n446\t        version_checker,\n447\t    )?;\n448\t\n449\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n450\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n451\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n452\t        // Filter by version floor, then rotate by query_seq\n453\t        let eligible: Vec<_> = replicas\n454\t            .iter()\n455\t            .filter(|node_id| {\n456\t                let version = version_checker(index, node_id.as_str());\n457\t                version >= floor\n458\t            })\n459\t            .collect();\n460\t\n461\t        if eligible.is_empty() {\n462\t            return None;\n463\t        }\n464\t\n465\t        let selected = eligible[query_seq as usize % eligible.len()];\n466\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected.clone());\n467\t    }\n468\t\n469\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n470\t        chosen_group,\n471\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n472\t        shard_to_node,\n473\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n474\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n475\t    })\n476\t}\n477\t\n478\t/// Plan search scatter for a specific replica group (plan §13.6 session pinning).\n479\t///\n480\t/// Used when a session has a pending write and needs to read from the pinned group\n481\t/// to ensure read-your-writes consistency.\n482\tpub fn plan_search_scatter_for_group(\n483\t    topology: &Topology,\n484\t    query_seq: u64,\n485\t    rf: usize,\n486\t    shard_count: u32,\n487\t    pinned_group: u32,\n488\t) -> Option<ScatterPlan> {\n489\t    let group = topology.group(pinned_group)?;\n490\t\n491\t    let mut shard_to_node = HashMap::new();\n492\t    for shard_id in 0..shard_count {\n493\t        let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), rf);\n494\t        if replicas.is_empty() {\n495\t            continue;\n496\t        }\n497\t        let selected = replicas[query_seq as usize % replicas.len()].clone();\n498\t        shard_to_node.insert(shard_id, selected);\n499\t    }\n500\t\n501\t    Some(ScatterPlan {\n502\t        chosen_group: pinned_group,\n503\t        target_shards: (0..shard_count).collect(),\n504\t        shard_to_node,\n505\t        deadline_ms: 5000,\n506\t        hedging_eligible: group.node_count() > 1,\n507\t    })\n508\t}\n509\t\n510\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count))]\n511\tpub async fn execute_scatter<C: NodeClient>(\n512\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n513\t    client: &C,\n514\t    req: SearchRequest,\n515\t    topology: &Topology,\n516\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n517\t) -> Result<ScatterResult> {\n518\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n519\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n520\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n521\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n522\t        }\n523\t    }\n524\t    tracing::Span::current().record(\"node_count\", node_to_shards.len());\n525\t\n526\t    let mut shard_pages = Vec::new();\n527\t    let mut failed_shards = HashMap::new();\n528\t    let mut deadline_exceeded = false;\n529\t\n530\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n531\t    for (node_id, shards) in node_to_shards {\n532\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n533\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n534\t            None => {\n535\t                for shard_id in shards {\n536\t                    failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::NetworkError(\"node not in topology\".to_string()));\n537\t                }\n538\t                continue;\n539\t            }\n540\t        };\n541\t        let client_ref = client;\n542\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n543\t        let node_id_clone = node_id.clone();\n544\t        let shard_count = shards.len();\n545\t        // Create a span for this node's scatter call\n546\t        let span = info_span!(\n547\t            \"scatter_node\",\n548\t            node_id = %node_id_clone,\n549\t            address = %node.address,\n550\t            shard_count = shard_count,\n551\t        );\n552\t        tasks.push(async move {\n553\t            let result = client_ref.search_node(&node_id_clone, &node.address, &req_clone).await;\n554\t            (node_id_clone, shards, result)\n555\t        }.instrument(span));\n556\t    }\n557\t\n558\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n559\t\n560\t    for (_node_id, shards, result) in results {\n561\t        match result {\n562\t            Ok(body) => {\n563\t                for _shard_id in shards {\n564\t                    shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body: body.clone() });\n565\t                }\n566\t            }\n567\t            Err(NodeError::Timeout) => {\n568\t                deadline_exceeded = true;\n569\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, NodeError::Timeout); }\n570\t            }\n571\t            Err(e) => {\n572\t                for shard_id in shards { failed_shards.insert(shard_id, e.clone()); }\n573\t            }\n574\t        }\n575\t    }\n576\t\n577\t    let partial = !failed_shards.is_empty();\n578\t\n579\t    match policy {\n580\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Error => {\n581\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n582\t                return Err(crate::error::MiroirError::Routing(format!(\"{} shard(s) unavailable\", failed_shards.len())));\n583\t            }\n584\t        }\n585\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial => {}\n586\t        UnavailableShardPolicy::Fallback => {\n587\t            // Group-unavailability fallback: try other replica groups for failed shards\n588\t            if !failed_shards.is_empty() {\n589\t                let mut fallback_pages = Vec::new();\n590\t                let mut remaining_failed = HashMap::new();\n591\t\n592\t                for (&shard_id, error) in &failed_shards {\n593\t                    let mut fallback_succeeded = false;\n594\t\n595\t                    // Try each other replica group\n596\t                    for group_id in 0..topology.replica_group_count() {\n597\t                        if group_id == plan.chosen_group {\n598\t                            continue; // Skip the already-tried group\n599\t                        }\n600\t\n601\t                        if let Some(group) = topology.group(group_id) {\n602\t                            let replicas = crate::router::assign_shard_in_group(shard_id, group.nodes(), topology.rf());\n603\t                            if replicas.is_empty() {\n604\t                                continue;\n605\t                            }\n606\t\n607\t                            // Try each replica in the fallback group\n608\t                            for node_id in replicas {\n609\t                                if let Some(node) = topology.node(&node_id) {\n610\t                                    match client.search_node(&node_id, &node.address, &req).await {\n611\t                                        Ok(body) => {\n612\t                                            fallback_pages.push(ShardHitPage { body });\n613\t                                            fallback_succeeded = true;\n614\t                                            break;\n615\t                                        }\n616\t                                        Err(_) => {\n617\t                                            continue; // Try next replica\n618\t                                        }\n619\t                                    }\n620\t                                }\n621\t                            }\n622\t\n623\t                            if fallback_succeeded {\n624\t                                break; // Found a working replica\n625\t                            }\n626\t                        }\n627\t                    }\n628\t\n629\t                    if !fallback_succeeded {\n630\t                        remaining_failed.insert(shard_id, error.clone());\n631\t                    }\n632\t                }\n633\t\n634\t                // Merge fallback results\n635\t                shard_pages.extend(fallback_pages);\n636\t\n637\t                // Update failed_shards with only those that truly failed\n638\t                if remaining_failed.is_empty() {\n639\t                    // All shards succeeded via fallback\n640\t                    return Ok(ScatterResult {\n641\t                        shard_pages,\n642\t                        failed_shards: HashMap::new(),\n643\t                        partial: false,\n644\t                        deadline_exceeded,\n645\t                    });\n646\t                } else {\n647\t                    // Some shards still failed\n648\t                    failed_shards = remaining_failed;\n649\t                }\n650\t            }\n651\t        }\n652\t    }\n653\t\n654\t    Ok(ScatterResult { shard_pages, failed_shards, partial, deadline_exceeded })\n655\t}\n656\t\n657\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n658\tpub async fn scatter_gather_search<C: NodeClient>(\n659\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n660\t    client: &C,\n661\t    req: SearchRequest,\n662\t    topology: &Topology,\n663\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n664\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n665\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n666\t    let scatter_result = execute_scatter(plan, client, req.clone(), topology, policy).await?;\n667\t\n668\t    let mut shard_pages = scatter_result.shard_pages;\n669\t    if scatter_result.partial {\n670\t        for shard_id in scatter_result.failed_shards.keys() {\n671\t            shard_pages.push(ShardHitPage {\n672\t                body: serde_json::json!({\"success\": false, \"message\": format!(\"shard {} unavailable\", shard_id)}),\n673\t            });\n674\t        }\n675\t    }\n676\t\n677\t    // Collect failed shard IDs for the X-Miroir-Degraded header\n678\t    let failed_shards: Vec<u32> = scatter_result.failed_shards.keys().copied().collect();\n679\t\n680\t    let merge_input = MergeInput {\n681\t        shard_hits: shard_pages,\n682\t        offset: req.offset,\n683\t        limit: req.limit,\n684\t        client_requested_score: req.ranking_score,\n685\t        facets: req.facets.clone(),\n686\t        failed_shards,\n687\t    };\n688\t\n689\t    // Span for the merge operation\n690\t    let _span = info_span!(\n691\t        \"merge\",\n692\t        shard_count = merge_input.shard_hits.len(),\n693\t        offset = req.offset,\n694\t        limit = req.limit,\n695\t    ).entered();\n696\t\n697\t    strategy.merge(merge_input)\n698\t}\n699\t\n700\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n701\t// OP#4: Global-IDF preflight execution\n702\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n703\t\n704\t/// Extract unique query terms from a search query string.\n705\tpub fn extract_query_terms(query: &Option<String>) -> Vec<String> {\n706\t    match query {\n707\t        Some(q) if !q.is_empty() => {\n708\t            let mut seen = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n709\t            let mut terms = Vec::new();\n710\t            for term in q.split_whitespace() {\n711\t                let lower = term.to_lowercase();\n712\t                if seen.insert(lower.clone()) { terms.push(lower); }\n713\t            }\n714\t            terms\n715\t        }\n716\t        _ => Vec::new(),\n717\t    }\n718\t}\n719\t\n720\t/// Execute the preflight phase: gather term frequencies from all shards.\n721\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(node_count, term_count = req.terms.len()))]\n722\tpub async fn execute_preflight<C: NodeClient>(\n723\t    plan: &ScatterPlan,\n724\t    client: &C,\n725\t    req: &PreflightRequest,\n726\t    topology: &Topology,\n727\t) -> Result<GlobalIdf> {\n728\t    if req.terms.is_empty() {\n729\t        return Ok(GlobalIdf { total_docs: 0, avg_doc_length: 0.0, terms: HashMap::new() });\n730\t    }\n731\t\n732\t    let mut node_to_shards: HashMap<NodeId, Vec<u32>> = HashMap::new();\n733\t    for (&shard_id, node_id) in &plan.shard_to_node {\n734\t        if plan.target_shards.contains(&shard_id) {\n735\t            node_to_shards.entry(node_id.clone()).or_default().push(shard_id);\n736\t        }\n737\t    }\n738\t\n739\t    let mut tasks = Vec::new();\n740\t    for (node_id, _) in node_to_shards {\n741\t        let node = match topology.node(&node_id) {\n742\t            Some(n) => n.clone(),\n743\t            None => continue,\n744\t        };\n745\t        let client_ref = client;\n746\t        let req_clone = req.clone();\n747\t        let nid = node_id.clone();\n748\t        tasks.push(async move { client_ref.preflight_node(&nid, &node.address, &req_clone).await });\n749\t    }\n750\t\n751\t    let results = futures_util::future::join_all(tasks).await;\n752\t    let responses: Vec<PreflightResponse> = results.into_iter().filter_map(|r| r.ok()).collect();\n753\t    Ok(GlobalIdf::from_preflight_responses(&responses))\n754\t}\n755\t\n756\t/// Execute a full dfs_query_then_fetch search (OP#4 global-IDF preflight).\n757\t#[instrument(skip_all, fields(index = %req.index_uid))]\n758\tpub async fn dfs_query_then_fetch_search<C: NodeClient>(\n759\t    plan: ScatterPlan,\n760\t    client: &C,\n761\t    req: SearchRequest,\n762\t    topology: &Topology,\n763\t    policy: UnavailableShardPolicy,\n764\t    strategy: &dyn MergeStrategy,\n765\t) -> Result<MergedSearchResult> {\n766\t    let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n767\t        index_uid: req.index_uid.clone(),\n768\t        terms: extract_query_terms(&req.query),\n769\t        filter: req.filter.clone(),\n770\t    };\n771\t    let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, client, &preflight_req, topology).await?;\n772\t    let mut search_req = req;\n773\t    search_req.global_idf = Some(global_idf);\n774\t    scatter_gather_search(plan, client, search_req, topology, policy, strategy).await\n775\t}\n776\t\n777\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n778\t// Mock client\n779\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n780\t\n781\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]\n782\tpub struct MockNodeClient {\n783\t    pub responses: HashMap<NodeId, Value>,\n784\t    pub preflight_responses: HashMap<NodeId, PreflightResponse>,\n785\t    pub write_responses: HashMap<NodeId, WriteResponse>,\n786\t    pub fetch_responses: HashMap<NodeId, FetchDocumentsResponse>,\n787\t    pub errors: HashMap<NodeId, NodeError>,\n788\t    pub delay_ms: u64,\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\timpl NodeClient for MockNodeClient {\n792\t    async fn search_node(\n793\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &SearchRequest,\n794\t    ) -> std::result::Result<Value, NodeError> {\n795\t        let _ = self.delay_ms;\n796\t        if \"estimatedTotalHits\": 0, \"processingTimeMs\": 0, \"facetDistribution\": {}})\n799\t        }))\n800\t    }\n801\t\n802\t    async fn preflight_node(\n803\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: Ok(self.preflight_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n807\t            PreflightResponse { total_docs: 1000, avg_doc_length: 50.0, term_stats: HashMap::new() }\n808\t        }))\n809\t    }\n810\t\n811\t    async fn write_documents(\n812\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &WriteRequest,\n813\t    ) -> std::result::Result<WriteResponse, NodeError> {\n814\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n815\t        Ok(self.write_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n816\t            WriteResponse {\n817\t   }))\n824\t    }\n825\t\n826\t    async fn delete_documents(\n827\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByIdsRequest,\n828\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n829\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n830\t        Ok(DeleteResponse {\n831\t            success: true,\n832\t            task_uid: Some(1),\n833\t            message: None,\n834\t            code: None,\n835\t            error_type: None,\n836\t        })\n837\t    }\n838\t\n839\t    async fn delete_documents_by_filter(\n840\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, _request: &DeleteByFilterRequest,\n841\t    ) -> std::result::Result<DeleteResponse, NodeError> {\n842\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) {            error_type: None,\n849\t        })\n850\t    }\n851\t\n852\t    async fn fetch_documents(\n853\t        &self, node: &NodeId, _address: &str, request: &FetchDocumentsRequest,\n854\t    ) -> std::result::Result<FetchDocumentsResponse, NodeError> {\n855\t        if let Some(err) = self.errors.get(node) { return Err(err.clone()); }\n856\t        // Return stored fetch response if available, otherwise return empty\n857\t        let stored = self.fetch_responses.get(node).cloned().unwrap_or_else(|| {\n858\t            FetchDocumentsResponse {\n859\t                results: Vec::new(),\n860\t                limit: request.limit,\n861\t                offset: request.offset,\n862\t                total: 0,\n863\t            }\n864\t        });\n865\t\n866\t        // Handle pagination: if offset exceeds total, return empty results\n867\t        if request.offset as u64 >= stored.total {\n868\t            return Ok(FetchDocumentsResponse {\n869\t                results: Vec::new(),\n870\t                limit: request.limit,\n871\t                offset: request.offset,\n872\t                total: stored.total,\n873\t            });\n874\t        }\n875\t\n876\t        Ok(stored)\n877\t    }\n878\t\n879\t    fn get_task_status(\n880\t        &self,\n881\t        node: &NodeId,\n882\t        _address: &str,\n883\t        _request: &TaskStatusRequest,\n884\t    ) -> impl std::future::Future<Output = std::result::Result<TaskStatusResponse, NodeError>> + Send {\n885\t        let node = node.clone();\n886\t        let task_uid = _request.task_uid;\n887\t        let error = self.errors.get(&node).cloned();\n888\t        async move {\n889\t            if let Some(err) = error {\n890\t                return Err(err);\n891\t            }\n892\t            Ok(TaskStatusResponse {\n893\t                task_uid,\n894\t                status: \"succeeded\".to_string(),\n895\t                error: None,\n896\t                error_type: None,\n897\t            })\n898\t        }\n899\t    }\n900\t}\n901\t\n902\t#[cfg(test)]\n903\tmod tests {\n904\t    use super::*;\n905\t    use crate::topology::{Node, plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 2, 64);\n1076\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1077\t        c.responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), serde_json::json!({\"hits\": [{\"id\": \"a\", \"_rankingScore\": 0.9}], \"estimatedTotalHits\": 1, \"processingTimeMs\": 5}));\n1078\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1079\t            total_docs: 50000, avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1080\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([(\"test\".into(), TermStats { df: 500 })]),\n1081\t        });\n1082\t        let s = crate::merger::RrfStrategy::default_strategy();\n1083\t        let r = dfs_query_then_fetch_search(plan, &c, make_req(), &topo, UnavailableShardPolicy::Partial, &s).await.unwrap();\n1084\t        assert!(!r.degraded);\n1085\t        assert!(!r.hits.is_empty());\n1086\t    }\n1087\t\n1088\t    /// Integration test for dfs_query_then_fetch with severely skewed shard distribution.\n1089\t    ///\n1090\t    /// This test simulates the scenario described in miroir-yio:\n1091\t    /// - Shard 0: Normal (1,000 docs, term df = 100)\n1092\t    /// - Shard 1: 100x normal (100,000 docs, term df = 10,000)\n1093\t    /// - Shard 2: Near-empty (10 docs, term df = 1)\n1094\t    ///\n1095\t    /// Without global IDF preflight, each shard would compute different local IDF values:\n1096\t    /// - Shard 0: IDF ≈ log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1097\t    /// - Shard 1: IDF ≈ log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1098\t    /// - Shard 2: IDF ≈ log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.8\n1099\t    ///\n1100\t    /// With global IDF preflight, all shards use the same IDF:\n1101\t    /// - Global: N = 101,010, df = 10,101\n1102\t    /// - IDF ≈ log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.3\n1103\t    ///\n1104\t    /// This ensures scores are comparable across shards, enabling correct score-based merge.\n1105\t    #[tokio::test]\n1106\t    async fn test_dfs_skewed_shards_global_idf_aggregation() {\n1107\t        let mut topo = Topology::new(3, 1, 1);\n1108\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), \"http://node-0:7700\".into(), 0));\n1109\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), \"http://node-1:7700\".into(), 0));\n1110\t        topo.add_node(Node::new(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), \"http://node-2:7700\".into(), 0));\n1111\t\n1112\t        let plan = plan_search_scatter(&topo, 0, 1, 3);\n1113\t\n1114\t        // Simulate severely skewed shard distribution\n1115\t        let mut c = MockNodeClient::default();\n1116\t\n1117\t        // Shard 0: Normal distribution\n1118\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-0\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1119\t            total_docs: 1000,\n1120\t            avg_doc_length: 50.0,\n1121\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1122\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 100 }),\n1123\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 80 }),\n1124\t            ]),\n1125\t        });\n1126\t\n1127\t        // Shard 1: 100x normal (severely skewed)\n1128\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-1\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1129\t            total_docs: 100_000,\n1130\t            avg_doc_length: 55.0,\n1131\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1132\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 10_000 }),\n1133\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 8_000 }),\n1134\t            ]),\n1135\t        });\n1136\t\n1137\t        // Shard 2: Near-empty\n1138\t        c.preflight_responses.insert(NodeId::new(\"node-2\".into()), PreflightResponse {\n1139\t            total_docs: 10,\n1140\t            avg_doc_length: 45.0,\n1141\t            term_stats: HashMap::from([\n1142\t                (\"machine\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1143\t                (\"learning\".into(), TermStats { df: 1 }),\n1144\t            ]),\n1145\t        });\n1146\t\n1147\t        // Execute preflight to get global IDF\n1148\t        let preflight_req = PreflightRequest {\n1149\t            index_uid: \"test\".into(),\n1150\t            terms: vec![\"machine\".into(), \"learning\".into()],\n1151\t            filter: None,\n1152\t        };\n1153\t\n1154\t        let global_idf = execute_preflight(&plan, &c, &preflight_req, &topo).await.unwrap();\n1155\t\n1156\t        // Verify global aggregation\n1157\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.total_docs, 101_010);\n1158\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().df, 10_101);\n1159\t        assert_eq!(global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().df, 8_081);\n1160\t\n1161\t        // Verify global IDF is the same for all shards\n1162\t        // Expected IDF for \"machine\": log((101010 - 10101 + 0.5) / (10101 + 0.5)) + 1\n1163\t        let expected_idf_machine: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 10101.0 + 0.5) / (10101.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1164\t        let actual_idf_machine = global_idf.terms.get(\"machine\").unwrap().idf;\n1165\t        assert!((actual_idf_machine - expected_idf_machine).abs() < 0.001);\n1166\t\n1167\t        // Expected IDF for \"learning\": log((101010 - 8081 + 0.5) / (8081 + 0.5)) + 1\n1168\t        let expected_idf_learning: f64 = ((101010.0_f64 - 8081.0 + 0.5) / (8081.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1169\t        let actual_idf_learning = global_idf.terms.get(\"learning\").unwrap().idf;\n1170\t        assert!((actual_idf_learning - expected_idf_learning).abs() < 0.001);\n1171\t\n1172\t        // Verify that without global IDF, local IDF values would differ significantly\n1173\t        // Shard 0 local IDF for \"machine\": log((1000 - 100 + 0.5) / (100 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.19\n1174\t        // Shard 1 local IDF for \"machine\": log((100000 - 10000 + 0.5) / (10000 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 3.20\n1175\t        // Shard 2 local IDF for \"machine\": log((10 - 1 + 0.5) / (1 + 0.5)) + 1 ≈ 2.85\n1176\t        let local_idf_shard_0: f64 = ((1000.0_f64 - 100.0 + 0.5) / (100.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1177\t        let local_idf_shard_2: f64 = ((10.0_f64 - 1.0 + 0.5) / (1.0 + 0.5)).ln() + 1.0;\n1178\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - local_idf_shard_0).abs() > 0.2, \"Local IDF values should differ significantly\");\n1179\t        assert!((local_idf_shard_2 - actual_idf_machine).abs() > 0.3, \"Global IDF should be closer to large-shard local IDF\");\n1180\t    }\n1181\t\n1182\t    /// Test that DFS preflight handles empty query terms gracefully.\n1183\t Orchestrator selects a **replica group** for this query: `group = query_sequence_number % RG` (round-robin ensures even load across groups). The `query_sequence_number` is a per-pod counter, not a cluster-wide one — under the multi-pod deployment of §14, cluster-wide balance across groups therefore depends on the Kubernetes Service load-balancer distributing incoming requests uniformly across Miroir pods (which it does for the default random / round-robin kube-proxy policies). See §14.4.\ndocs/plan/plan.md:488:pub fn query_group(query_seq: u64, replica_groups: u32) -> u32 {\ndocs/plan/plan.md:489:    (query_seq % replica_groups as u64) as u32\ndocs/plan/plan.md:493:pub fn covering_set(shard_count: u32, group: &Group, rf: usize, query_seq: u64) -> Vec<NodeId> {\ndocs/plan/plan.md:497:        replicas[(query_seq as usize) % replicas.len()].clone()\ndocs/plan/plan.md:2325:Replaces the `query_seq`-based round-robin in `covering_set` (Section 2). update_latency(&mut self, latency_ms: f64) {\n114\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n115\t        self.latency_p95_ms = alpha * self.latency_p95_ms + (1.0 - alpha) * latency_ms;\n116\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n117\t    }\n118\t\n119\t    /// Update error rate with EWMA smoothing.\n120\t    pub fn update_error(&mut self, is_error: bool) {\n121\t        let alpha = 0.5_f64.powf((self.half_life_ms as f64) / 1000.0);\n122\t        let new_error = if is_error { 1.0 } else { 0.0 };\n123\t        self.error_rate = alpha * self.error_rate + (1.0 - alpha) * new_error;\n124\t        self.last_updated = Instant::now();\n125\t    }\n126\t\n127\t    /// Increment in-flight count.\n128\t    pub fn increment_in_flight(&mut self) {\n129\t        self.in_flight += 1;\n130\t    }\n131\t\n132\t    /// Decrement in-flight count.\n133\t    pub fn decrement_in_flight(&mut self) {\n134\t        self.in_flight = self.in_flight.saturating_sub(1);\n135\t    }\n136\t\n137\t    /// Compute the composite score (lower is better).\n138\t    pub fn score(&self, config: &ReplicaSelectionConfig) -> f64 {\n139\t        config.latency_weight * self.latency_p95_ms\n140\t            + config.inflight_weight * (self.in_flight as f64)\n141\t            + config.error_weight * (self.error_rate * 1000.0)\n142\t    }\n143\t}\n144\t\n145\timpl Default for NodeMetrics {\n146\t    fn default() -> Self {\n147\t        Self::new(50.0, 5000)\n148\t    }\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t/// Replica selector.\n152\tpub struct Self {\n167\t            config,\n168\t            metrics: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n169\t            rr_counter: Arc::new(RwLock::new(HashMap::new())),\n170\t            rng: ID, or None if candidates is empty.\n177\t    pub async fn select(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u32) -> Option<NodeId> {\n178\t        if candidates.is_empty() {\n179\t            return None;\n180\t        }\n181\t\n182\t        let strategy = self.parse_strategy();\n183\t\n184\t        match strategy {\n185\t            SelectionStrategy::Adaptive => self.select_adaptive(candidates).await,\n186\t            SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin => self.select_round_robin(candidates, group_id as u64).await,\n187\t            SelectionStrategy::Random => self.select_random(candidates),\n188\t        }\n189\t    }\n190\t\n191\t    /// Adaptive selection using EWMA scores.\n192\t    async fn select_adaptive(&self, candidates: &[NodeId]) -> Option<NodeId> {\n193\t        let metrics = self.metrics.read().await;\n194\t\n195\t        // Exploration: with probability epsilon, pick randomly\n196\t        if self.should_explore() {\n197\t            return self.select_random(candidates);\n198\t        }\n199\t\n200\t        // Compute scores and find the minimum\n201\t        let mut best_node = None;\n202\t        let mut best_score = f64::INFINITY;\n203\t\n204\t        for node in candidates {\n205\t            let score = metrics\n206\t                .get(node)\n207\t                .map(|m| m.score(&self.config))\n208\t                .unwrap_or(1000.0); // High default for unknown nodes\n209\t\n210\t            if score < best_score {\n211\t                best_score = score;\n212\t                best_node = Some(node.clone());\n213\t            }\n214\t        }\n215\t\n216\t        best_node\n217\t    }\n218\t\n219\t    /// Round-robin selection.\n220\t    async fn select_round_robin(&self, candidates: &[NodeId], group_id: u64) -> Option<NodeId> {\n221\t        let key = format!(\"group_{}\", group_id);\n222\t        let mut counter = self.rr_counter.write().await;\n223\t        let idx = *counter.entry(key.clone()).or_insert(0) as usize % candidates.len();\n224\t        *counter.get_mut(&key).unwrap() += 1;\n225\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n226\t    }\n227\t\n228\t    /// idx = self\n234\t            .rng\n235\t            .lock()\n236\t            .unwrap()\n237\t            .gen_range(0..candidates.len());\n238\t        Some(candidates[idx].clone())\n239\t    }\n240\t\n241\t    /// Check if we should explore (random selection).\n242\t    fn should_explore(&self) -> bool {\n243\t        let mut rng = self.rng.lock().unwrap();\n244\t        rng.gen::<f64>() < self.config.exploration_epsilon\n245\t    }\n246\t\n247\t    /// Record a successful request (update latency).\n248\t    pub async fn record_success(&self, node: &NodeId, latency_ms: f64) {\n249\t        let mut metrics = self.metrics.write().await;\n250\t        let entry = metrics\n251\t            .entry(node.clone())\n252\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n253\t        entry.update_latency(latency_ms);\n254\t        entry.update_error(false);\n255\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n256\t    }\n257\t\n258\t    /// Record a failed request.\n259\t    pub async fn .entry(node.clone())\n263\t            .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n264\t        if let Some(lat) = latency_ms {\n265\t            entry.update_latency(lat);\n266\t        }\n267\t        entry.update_error(true);\n268\t        entry.decrement_in_flight();\n269\t    }\n270\t\n271\t    /// Record that a request is being sent to a node.\n272\t    pub async fn record_request_start(&self, .or_insert_with(NodeMetrics::default);\n277\t        entry.increment_in_flight();\n278\t    }\n279\t\n280\t    /// Get metrics for a node.\n281\t    pub async fn get_metrics(&self, node: &NodeId) -> fn parse_strategy(&self) -> SelectionStrategy {\n288\t        match self.config.strategy.as_str() {\n289\t            \"round_robin\" => SelectionStrategy::RoundRobin,\n290\t            \"random\" =>
